coldStandardsFound.title: § 1910.28 Duty to have fall protection and falling object protection.
§ 1910.29 Fall protection systems and falling object protection—criteria and practices.

evidenceNeededDraft: Identify the specific platform or workplace location involved.
Confirm whether physical or administrative safety controls for fall_from_height are in place and operational.
Review training records, inspection checklists, or maintenance documentation relevant to walking_working_surfaces.
Verify conformance with standard citations: 29 CFR 1910.22, 29 CFR 1910.23, 29 CFR 1910.28, 29 CFR 1910.29, 29 CFR 1910.140.
[Advisory Only] Qualified safety personnel must verify specific evidence requirements on-site.
correctiveActionPatternsDraft: Restrict access to or isolate any hazardous conditions related to fall_from_height immediately.
Inspect, repair, or replace any defective components of the affected platform before returning to service.
Implement engineering, administrative, or PPE controls to minimize risks from fall_from_height.
Instruct workers on safe practices regarding walking_working_surfaces and verify procedural compliance.
Document corrective actions completed and ensure compliance with citations: 29 CFR 1910.22, 29 CFR 1910.23, 29 CFR 1910.28, 29 CFR 1910.29, 29 CFR 1910.140.
[Advisory Only] Corrective action details must be reviewed and approved by qualified safety managers or engineers.
applicabilityDraft: Applies to operations within jurisdiction: osha_general_industry.
Applies when task, activity, or condition involves platform or associated subsystems.
Applies when workers are exposed to fall_from_height risks during normal or maintenance operations.
[Advisory Only] Scope of applicability must be validated by a qualified inspector or safety professional.
reviewerNotes: Draft candidate generated for backlog item: "OSHA general industry walking-working surfaces and fall protection". Priority: critical. Backlog Status: source_ingestion_needed. Requires qualified human review and approval.
